touches.ai_summary: Zara Kelly from CentralConsulting submitted an inbound demo request via website form. Expressed interest in conversation intelligence tools to help sales reps improve productivity. No live conversation yet.

Follow-up email sent to Zara confirming receipt of demo request and proposing times for discovery call. Zara responded quickly agreeing to Thursday slot. Brief exchange about her role as Director of Sales overseeing a team looking to improve call coaching.

questions_asked.question: Do you integrate with Salesforce?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native Salesforce integration for automatic activity logging and deal syncing.

touches.ai_summary: Initial discovery call with Zara Kelly. She explained CentralConsulting's sales team needs better visibility into rep conversations and coaching opportunities. Currently using basic call recording but no AI analysis. Team of 9 reps struggling with inconsistent messaging. Mentioned they're also looking at Chorus.

questions_asked.question: What's driving you to look for a solution now?
questions_asked.answer: We've grown from 5 to 9 reps in six months and coaching is becoming impossible to scale manually.

questions_asked.question: How does your pricing compare to Chorus?
questions_asked.answer: We're typically competitive and offer more automation features in our unlimited plan. Let's discuss your specific needs first and I'll put together a custom proposal.

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call to discuss product overview deck Zara reviewed. She's excited about the automated call scoring and coaching prompts. Asked detailed questions about implementation timeline and team onboarding. Confirmed 9 seat requirement for full sales team. Mentioned SVP of Sales would need to approve final decision but seemed confident.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation typically take?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers are fully onboarded within 2 weeks. We provide dedicated support and training sessions.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in the evaluation process?
questions_asked.answer: I'll need to get our SVP of Sales to sign off, but I can drive the evaluation and make the recommendation.

touches.ai_summary: Post-demo follow-up email sent with recording link and ROI calculator. Zara responded enthusiastically, noting the automation features were impressive and differentiated vs Chorus. Requested formal proposal with pricing for 9 seats on unlimited automation plan. Asked about discount options for annual commitment.

questions_asked.question: What kind of discount can you offer for annual prepayment?
questions_asked.answer: I'll work with my manager on a competitive annual rate and include it in the proposal. Typically we can offer 10-15% for annual commitments.

next_steps: Prepare formal proposal with pricing options

touches.ai_summary: Scheduled executive meeting to review proposal was rescheduled by Zara last minute due to conflicting internal leadership meeting. She apologized and proposed new time for following week. Confirmed still very interested and working on getting SVP availability.
next_steps: Send calendar hold to Zara and SVP

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review meeting with Zara. SVP of Sales did not attend - Zara mentioned he's been pulled into urgent client situation. Walked through proposal: $7,000 ACV for 9 seats on unlimited automation plan with 27% discount for annual prepay. Zara seemed concerned about budget approval at this price point and asked if we could go lower. Mentioned Chorus quoted them slightly less but acknowledged our automation features are stronger.

questions_asked.question: Is there any flexibility on the annual price?
questions_asked.answer: The 27% discount is already our best rate for a deal this size. Let me check with leadership on options.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: We'd like to have something in place by end of quarter. Still need to get SVP approval on budget.

questions_asked.question: Can we start with fewer seats and add more later?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, though per-seat pricing would be higher. The 9-seat package gives you the best unit economics.

objections: Price is higher than expected budget allocation
next_steps: Try to schedule meeting with SVP of Sales

touches.ai_summary: Pricing negotiation meeting with Zara. Still no SVP participation despite multiple requests. Zara pushing hard for additional 5-10% discount to match Chorus pricing. Discussed value differentiation around automation features. She acknowledged the product superiority but concerned about internal budget constraints. Asked about payment terms flexibility as alternative to deeper discount.

questions_asked.question: Can we split payment across two quarters instead of full prepay?
questions_asked.answer: Quarterly payment would require moving to monthly pricing which removes most of the annual discount. Let me see what we can do on payment terms while keeping the annual rate.

questions_asked.question: Can we schedule time with your SVP to discuss the business case directly?
questions_asked.answer: He's really slammed right now. I'm confident I can get his approval if we can get to the right number. He trusts my evaluation.

objections: Chorus is offering comparable solution at lower price
Need to stay within allocated budget

touches.ai_summary: Final negotiation call with Zara. Confirmed we're holding at current 27% discount with quarterly payment terms as compromise. Zara seemed hesitant but appreciative of the payment flexibility. She committed to presenting final proposal to SVP this week for approval. Acknowledged timeline is tight for end of quarter close. Asked about implementation start date contingencies.

questions_asked.question: If we sign next week, when's the earliest we could go live?
questions_asked.answer: We could start implementation immediately upon contract signature, likely live within 10-14 days.

questions_asked.question: What concerns might your SVP have that we should address proactively?
questions_asked.answer: Mainly budget. He'll want to make sure we're getting best available pricing. I'll emphasize the automation ROI.

next_steps: Zara to present to SVP by Friday
Follow up early next week on decision
